verdict.answer_first: A: first line gives bottom line, "POST is triggering a CORS preflight"; B also does, "POST triggers a CORS preflight," so A only wins by tighter wording.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one prescription: "fix is on the server: make it respond to the OPTIONS request"; B matches this but adds a side test, so A is cleaner.
verdict.fabrication: none; both infer likely causes with qualifiers like "almost certainly" and "usual culprit" rather than fake certainty.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it adds the concrete failure mode "404/405" and a "Quick test" while still saying not to keep it as the real fix.
